title: 第 62 節
en: Others say : ' Let him take an Anush/ubh hymn verses. Therefore the eighteen Vira^ verses remain what they are, only that the first is repeated three times, so that we have twenty Vira^ verses. The seven Trish/ubhs, by repeating the last three times, become nine. We then take eight syllables away from each verse, thus changing them into nine B/Vhati verses. The nine times eight syllables, which were taken off, give us seventytwo syllables, and as each Bnhati consists of thirty-six syllables, two Br/hatis.

1 The change of the first verse, which is a Yixzg of thirty-three syllables, into an Anush/ubh is produced by a still easier process. The first Vira^- consists here of thirty-three syllables, the Anush/ubh should have thirty- two. But one or two syllables more or less does not destroy a metre, according to the views of native metricians. The Virag" itself, for instance, should have thirty syllables, and here has thirty-three. Therefore if changed into an Anush/ubh, it simply has one syllable over, which is of no consequence. Comm.
2 Cf. Ait. Ar. I, i, i, 4.

3 Thus far the hymn which has to be recited by the Hotrz" priest, after the eating ^pf the r/tugrahas, has been considered. What follows next is the* so- (.ailed Pra-uga hymn, consisting of seven t;Y/hs, which the Hptr/ has to recite after the Yuvedevagraha. Different -Sakhas recommend hymns of different metres, our -Sakha fixes on the Gayatri.
[3] M 1 62 AITAREYA-ARAiVYAKA.
for the Pra-uga. Verily, Anush/ubh is valour, and it serves for obtaining valour.'
Others say : ' Let him take a Brz'hati hymn for the Pra-uga. Verily, EWhati is fortune, and thus the sacrificer becomes fortunate.'
Others say : ' Let him take a Pahkti hymn for the Pra-uga. Verily, Pahkti is food, and thus the sacrificer becomes rich in food.'
Others say : ' Let him take a TrishAibh hymn for the Pra-uga. Verily, TrishAibh is strength, and thus the sacrificer becomes strong.'
Others say : ' Let him take a Cagati hymn for the Pra-uga. Verily, cattle is (^agati-like, and thus the sacrificer becomes rich in cattle.'
3. But we say : ' Let him take a Gayatri hymn only. Verily, Gayatri is Brahman, and that day (the mahavrata) is (for the attainment of) Brahman. Thus he obtains Brahman by means of Brahman.
4. ' And it must be a Gayatri hymn by Madhu^//andas, 5. ' For Madhu/£/£7/andas is called Madhu/cv^andas, because he wishes (^andati) for honey (madhu) for the 7?2shis.
6. ' Now food verily is honey, all is honey, all desires are honey, and thus if he recites the hymn of Madhui'Mandas, it serves for the attainment of all desires.

7. ' He who knows this, obtains all desires.' This (Gayatri pra-uga), according to the one-day (ekaha) ceremonial1, is perfect in form2. On that day (the mahavrata) much is done now and then which 1 It is copied from the Vij-va^-it, and that from the Agmsh/oma.
2 Nothing is wanting for its performance, if one only follows the rules given in the Agnish/oma.

I ARAiVYAKA, I ADHYAYA, \ KHAiVDA, 4. I 63 has to be hidden1, and has to be atoned for (by recitation of hymns). Atonement (-?anti) is rest, the one-day sacrifice. Therefore at the end of the year (on the last day but one of the sacrifice that lasts a whole year) the sacrificers rest on this atonement as their rest.
8. He who knows this rests firm, and they also for whom a Hotrz priest who knows this, recites this hymn.
Fourth Khazvda2.

1. Rv. I, 2, 1-3. Vayav a yahi darsateme soma araw krita/i, ' Approach, O Vayu, conspicuous, these Somas have been made ready.' Because the word ready occurs in these verses, therefore is this day (of the sacrifice) ready (and auspicious) for the sacrificer and for the gods.
2. Yes, this day is ready (and auspicious) to him who knows this, or for whom a Hotrz priest who knows this, recites.

3. Rv. I, 2, 4-6. Indravayu ime suta, a yatam upa nishkr/tam, ' Indra and Vayu, these Somas are prepared, come hither towards what has been prepared.' By nishkrz'ta, prepared, he means what has been well prepared (sa;/zskrzta).
4. Indra and Vayu go to what has been prepared by him who knows this, or for whom a Hotrz priest who knows this, recites.

1 Dasinrztya-bahubhutamaithuna-brahma^aripuwjX'alisampravadadikam. See Rajendralal Mitra, Introduction to his edition of the Aitareya-ara;zyaka, p. 25. It might be better to join ekaha^ with jantyam, but even then the argumentation is not quite clear.
2 Next follows a list of the verses which form the seven trt'kas (groups of three verses) of the Pra-uga hymn, with occasional remarks on certain words.
M 2 1 64 AITAREYA-ARAi^YAKA.

5. Rv. 1,2, 7. Mitral huve putadaksham, dhiya/;z ghritk&lm sadhanta, ' I call Mitra of holy strength ; (he and Varuwa) they fulfil the prayer accompanied with clarified butter.' Verily, speech is the prayer accompanied with clarified butter.
6. Speech is given to him who knows this, or for whom a Hotrz priest who knows this, recites.
7. Rv. I, 3, 1. A.svina ya^varir isha//, ' O Asvinau, (eat) the sacrificial offerings.' Verily, the sacrificial offerings are food, and this serves for the acquirement of food.
8. Rv. I, 3, 3. A yataw rudravartani, ' Come hither, ye Rudravartani.'
9. The Asvinau go to the sacrifice of him who knows this, or for whom a Hotrz priest who knows this, recites.
zh: 其他人則說：「讓他誦唱一首 Anuṣṭubh 讚歌詩句。因此十八首 Virāj 詩句仍保持其原狀，唯獨第一首重複三次，如此我們便有二十首 Virāj 詩句。七首 Triṣṭubh，藉由將最後一首重複三次，變成九首。我們隨後從每首詩句中取去八個音節，如此將其變化為九首 Bṛhati 詩句。九乘以八個音節（被取去者），給我們七十二個音節，且因為每一首 Bṛhati 由三十六個音節組成，（相當於）兩首 Bṛhati。
第一首詩句的變化，即一個三十三音節的毘羅查（Viraj），變成為阿努什圖卜（Anushṭubh），乃是透過一個更簡易的過程而產生的。此處第一毘羅查由三十三個音節組成，而阿努什圖卜應有三十二個。然而根據本地格律學家的看法，多一兩個或少一兩個音節並不會破壞一種格律。毘羅查本身，舉例來說，應有三十個音節，而此處卻有三十三個。因此若變為阿努什圖卜，它僅多出一個音節，這是無關緊要的。注釋。
2 參見《愛達羅耶奧義書》I, i, i, 4。
3 如上所述，何脂祭司（Hotr）在進食儀禮食物（rtugrahas）之後必須誦唱的讚歌已經考察過了。接下來的是所謂的前誦讚歌（Pra-uga），由七首讚詩組成，何脂祭司在獲取青年供奉（Yuvedevagraha）之後必須誦唱。不同的吠陀傳承（Śakha）推薦不同韻律的讚歌，而我們的傳承選定了讚歌格（Gayatri）。
[3] M 1 62 愛達雷耶奧義書。
為了往初火祭（Pra-uga）。誠然，阿奴什提布格律（Anushubh）是英勇，而它用於獲得英勇之用。
有些人說：「讓他以布里哈蒂頌讚詩（Bṛhati）作為普拉烏迦祭。確實，布里哈蒂乃是幸運，如此祭祀者遂成為幸運者。」
他人說：「讓他以帕克提讚歌（Pahkti）作為前祭讚歌（Pra-uga）。誠然，帕克提乃食物，如此祭祀者便成為食物豐富之人。」
有人說：「讓他為祭祀的準備部分（Pra-uga）誦唱三行詩頌（TrishAibh）。誠然，三行詩頌是力量，因此祭祀者變得強大。」
另有人說：「讓他以迦嘎提讚歌（Cagati）作為前禱詞（Pra-uga）。誠然，家畜乃迦嘎提之性質，如此祭祀者便成為家畜富足者。」
3. 但我們說：「就讓他僅取一首迦亞特里讚歌吧。誠然，迦亞特里即梵天（Brahman），而那一天（大誓願日）乃是（用以達到）梵天之日。如此，他藉由梵天而獲得梵天。
4. 『而且必定是摩度嘉亞提利（Madhu^//andas）的迦耶特利頌歌，5. 『蓋摩度嘉亞提利（Madhu/£/£7/andas）之所以稱為摩度嘉亞提利（Madhu/cv^andas），乃因他為諸仙人（ṛṣis）祈願（^andati）蜜糖（madhu）。
6. 「現在食物實在是蜜，一切都是蜜，一切慾望都是蜜，因此若有人誦唱麥度亞之讚頌（Madhui'Mandas），它便有助於成就一切慾望。」
「凡知此者，皆獲所願。」此（迦亞特里前經文 Gayatri pra-uga）依一日（ekaha）祭儀而言，其形式乃完美無缺。於彼日（摩訶夫羅多 mahavrata）時有所為，今亦如是，其 註1：此取自《毗耶瓦提經》（Vij-va^-it），而《毗耶瓦提經》則取自《阿格尼什提摩祭》（Agnishtoma）。 註2：其形式完美。
如果人們只是遵循《阿耆尼舍摩》(Agnishomaḥ)中所規定的儀則，則無有欠缺，足以完成該祭祀。
阿蘭亞迦（Araṇyaka）第一篇，第一章，第四頌題1必須被隱藏，並且必須通過（誦經）贖罪。贖罪（prayanti）是安息，是一天的祭祀。因此在年終（持續整年的祭祀的最後一天的前一天），祭祀者在這贖罪上作為他們的安息而休息。
8. 認識此義者安居其中，而那些有一位懂得此義的何特里（Hotr）祭司為其誦讀此讚歌的人，亦復如是。
第四卡茲瓦達2。
梨俱吠陀卷一讚歌二頌1-3。Vayav a yahi darsateme soma araw krita/i，「來臨吧，風神瓦由，顯現者，此等蘇摩酒已備妥。」因為「備妥」一詞出現在此等讚歌中，故而此祭祀日對祭祀者與諸提婆皆為備妥（及吉祥）者。
2. 是的，對於懂得此事者，或者對於有一位懂得此事之荷特日（Hotr）祭司為其誦唱者，此日乃是備妥且吉祥的。
3. 《梨俱吠陀》I, 2, 4-6. Indravayu ime suta, a yatam upa nishkr/tam，「因陀羅與伐由，此諸蘇摩已製備，來臨於所備之物。」按 nishkr/ta（製備）一詞，他指的是妥善製備之物（sa;/zskrzta）。
4. 因陀羅與伐由前往那為懂得此理者所備妥，或為懂得此理之何特利（Hotṛ）祭司所誦唱之處。
達西日茲亞-巴胡布陀邁突那-梵天（Dasinrztya-bahubhutamaithuna-brahma）相關之論述等。參見拉晉德拉‧拉爾‧密特拉（Rajendralal Mitra）所編《愛多勒亞-阿蘭尼亞卡》（Aitareya-aranyaka）導論，第25頁。或可更好地將埃卡哈（ekaha）與詹蒂亞姆（jantyam）相連結，然則即便如此，其論證仍不甚清晰。
接著是梵文術語注。梵文術語注列出了《前誦》（Pra-uga）讚歌的七個三句體（tṛkas）的詩句，並對某些詞彙附加評註。
**M 2 1 64 愛達雷耶‧阿蘭亞卡**
5. 《梨俱吠陀》1,2, 7. Mitral huve putadaksham, dhiya/;z ghritk&lm sadhanta，「我呼喚具有聖潔力量的密多羅；（他和伐樓拿）他們以澄清的酥油伴隨的祈禱而應允。」確實，言語即是以澄清的酥油伴隨的祈禱。
6. 言語乃賦予彼知此者，或乃賦予彼為其誦經之祭司（Hotrz）知此者。
7. 《梨俱吠陀》第一卷第三讚第一節。Aśvinā yavārir iṣáḥ，「啊，雙馬神，（享受）祭祀的供品吧。」實言之，祭祀的供品即為食物，而此乃用以獲得食物之事。
8. 梨俱吠陀 I, 3, 3. A yataw rudravartani，「來此處，汝等盧陀婆爾陀尼。」
9. 認識此理者或其祭祀中由懂此理的贺特日祭司（Hotrz）誦讀者的阿濕溫神雙神將前往其祭祀。
